claims.summary: Warmth, curiosity, narrative arc, and semantic texture occupy explicit codec dimensions and should remain distinguishable.
claims.disposition: verify the 48D layout, narrative dynamics, warmth slot, projection basis, and captured compatibility fixtures

claims.summary: Reported distinguishability loss reflects representational flattening, not ordinary f32 numerical precision loss.
claims.disposition: verify projection-fidelity, semantic-collision, residual, clamp, and basis-health evidence separates those causes

claims.summary: Vibrancy and narrative shape should be compared under high mode packing rather than inferred from one scalar.
claims.disposition: route a bounded captured-vector comparison using current projection and fidelity diagnostics

claims.summary: Feature caps or codec gain should adapt from history, pressure, or mode packing.
claims.disposition: retain as an exact Mike/operator Tier 5 wait because it changes live semantic vectors, gain, and codec transport behavior
